Combined with SAM 3 (segmentation) and DINOv3, Meta now anchors
the academic CV stack the way it anchored NLP with Llama.

nodes.body_md:
## What's distinctive

- **No captions required** — pure self-supervised; learns visual structure from raw pixels
- **1.7B image dataset** — largest self-supervised vision pretraining to date
- **Gram anchoring** — novel technique to prevent feature collapse
- **ConvNeXt + ViT variants** — ships as a family, picks architecture per use case

nodes.title: Seedream 4.0 — ByteDance's MoE Image Speed King

model_spec.architecture: MoE diffusion / rectified-flow

public_view.plain_english:
ByteDance's August 2025 image model — generates 2K-resolution
images in 1.8 seconds via a mixture-of-experts architecture.
Speed is the differentiator: most quality-tier models take 5-15
seconds. Powers ByteDance's Doubao image generation and the
Jimeng creative app, plus is the image backbone for Seedance
video.
public_view.analogy:
Most image models are oil painters — careful, slow. Seedream is
a charcoal-sketch artist who can produce a finished piece in
seconds without losing detail.
public_view.investment_angle:
ByteDance's image+video stack (Seedream + Seedance + Hailuo
counterpart at MiniMax) is the basis of TikTok's coming
generative-video feature pipeline. Seedream's MoE speed makes
creator-tier real-time editing economically viable.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Demonstrated that MoE works for image diffusion as well as
language — same active-parameter trick. Seedream 4.5 / 5.0
refreshes have iterated quickly, and Seedance video is built on
the same architecture.

nodes.body_md:
## Chinese image-model landscape

| Model | Org | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Seedream 4.0 / 5.0 | ByteDance | Speed leader; MoE |
| Qwen-Image | Alibaba | Apache 2.0; best Chinese text rendering |
| HunyuanImage 3.0 | Tencent | 80B/13B-active MoE; largest open image model |
| Kolors / Kling Image | Kuaishou | Pre-Kling video stack |
| ERNIE-ViLG | Baidu | — |
| Jimeng | ByteDance | Consumer app on Seedream backbone |

nodes.title: Wan 2.x — Alibaba's Open-Source Video Family

model_spec.architecture: DiT (diffusion transformer) with Thinking Mode (2.7)

public_view.plain_english:
Alibaba's open-source video family — Wan 2.2 → 2.5 → 2.6 → 2.7 —
leads the open VBench leaderboard. Wan 2.7 (early 2026) added a
"Thinking Mode" that plans complex multi-shot videos before
generating, similar to Veo 3 Ingredients. Apache 2.0 throughout.
public_view.analogy:
What Qwen is to text, Wan is to video — Alibaba's fully-open
play in a closed-dominated market.
public_view.investment_angle:
Alibaba's open video play, parallel to Qwen's open text play.
Strategic value: anchor Bailian Cloud video offerings and prove
that Alibaba can compete with ByteDance (closed Seedance) on
technical merits without licensing fees.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Currently the strongest fully-open video model on VBench. Pushed
HunyuanVideo and other open competitors to keep up. Made open
video generation a real procurement option for media / agencies.

nodes.body_md:
## Wan timeline

| Version | Date | Key feature |
|---|---|---|
| Wan 2.0 | Mid 2024 | Initial release |
| Wan 2.2 | Late 2024 | 14B params, VBench leader |
| Wan 2.5 | Sep 2025 | Lip-sync + multi-shot |
| Wan 2.6 | Late 2025 | 1080p/30fps |
| Wan 2.7 | Early 2026 | Thinking Mode (plan-then-generate) |

nodes.title: Qwen3-VL — Open Vision-Language Frontier

model_spec.architecture: Qwen 3 + native vision encoder + Thinking variants

public_view.plain_english:
Alibaba's vision-enabled Qwen 3 family — sizes from 2B (mobile)
to 235B-A22B (frontier). Like the text Qwen 3, every variant has
a "Thinking" mode that turns on multimodal chain-of-thought
reasoning. Apache 2.0 throughout. Qwen2.5-VL remains widely used
as the smaller-cost VLM for production deployments.
public_view.analogy:
Qwen 3's eyes. Same brain, plus a vision encoder. Comes in every
size from a smartphone-fit 2B up to a frontier MoE.
public_view.investment_angle:
Vision-language is now table stakes for any LLM family. Qwen3-VL
is the strongest open VLM at the frontier scale, beating
Llama 4 Vision and competing with closed Pixtral Large. The
Apache 2.0 license + size range make it the default for
self-hosted vision RAG in enterprise.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Open vision-language at the 235B scale, with Thinking mode for
multimodal reasoning — closes the gap with closed multimodal
frontier (GPT-4o, Claude 4-series, Gemini 3) for enterprises
that require open weights.

nodes.body_md:
## Why VLM proliferation matters

By Q4 2025 every major lab ships a vision-language model:

| Lab | VLM | Open? |
|---|---|---|
| Anthropic | Claude (vision) | Closed |
| OpenAI | GPT-4o / GPT-5 | Closed |
| Google | Gemini 3 / PaliGemma 2 | Closed / Open |
| Meta | Llama 4 / Llama 3.2 Vision | Open |
| Alibaba | Qwen3-VL / Qwen2.5-Omni | Open |
| Mistral | Pixtral Large / 12B | Open |
| Microsoft | Phi-4-multimodal | Open |
| OpenGVLab | InternVL 3.5 | Open |
| Allen AI | Molmo / Molmo 2 | Open |

nodes.title: DeepSeek V3.2 — Sparse Attention at Scale

model_spec.architecture: MoE + DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA)

public_view.plain_english:
DeepSeek's late-2025 refresh of V3. The big change is "sparse
attention" — instead of every token comparing itself to every other
token, each token only attends to the most relevant ones. This
drops inference cost dramatically at long context, letting the
model handle 163K-token inputs without quadratic blow-up.
public_view.analogy:
Full attention is like every guest at a party speaking with every
other guest — fine for 10 people, exponentially expensive for
1,000. Sparse attention is "talk to the 30 people you care about" —
same conversation, vastly less audio.
public_view.investment_angle:
Long-context inference cost has been the wall for legal /
healthcare / agent workflows. DSA is the first production-scale
proof that you can break the n² attention bill without losing
quality. DeepSeek slashed prices ~50% with V3.2 — putting
further pressure on closed-API margins. The architecture is open;
expect Qwen, Llama, and Mistral followers in 2026.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Establishes sparse attention as a viable production path, not just
a research idea. Combined with R1's thinking and V3's MoE, V3.2 is
the third major architectural axis DeepSeek has industrialized in
a single year.

citations.title: DeepSeek-V3.2: Sparse Attention for Efficient Long-Context

nodes.body_md:
## What changed from V3

V3.2 keeps the 671B / 37B active MoE backbone. The new piece is **DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA)** — each query attends to a learned subset of keys rather than all of them. At 128K context, attention compute drops ~3-4× with negligible quality loss.

It also bakes "thinking" mode into the base model — no separate R1 distillation step needed.

## Why this matters strategically

DeepSeek has now shipped, in a single year:

1. **V3** — Multi-head Latent Attention + DeepSeekMoE (cheap MoE training)
2. **R1** — Pure-RL reasoning emergence at scale
3. **V3.2** — Sparse attention production deployment

Each is a published recipe. Each changes how the rest of the field builds. Combined, they pushed open-weight Chinese models from "behind" to "ahead on cost" in 12 months.

nodes.title: Sora 2 — Native Audio + Improved Physics

model_spec.architecture: DiT successor with native audio token generation + improved spatiotemporal coherence

benchmarks.name: Audio + video joint generation
benchmarks.score: first OpenAI release with synchronized AI-generated audio + visuals
benchmarks.vs_baseline: Sora 1 silent; competitors had cascaded audio (separate model)

public_view.plain_english:
OpenAI's September 2025 Sora successor — the first version
with native synchronized audio generation (sound effects,
speech, ambient audio matched to visuals) and substantially
improved physical consistency (objects fall correctly,
fluids flow plausibly, characters maintain identity across
cuts). Distributed initially through a TikTok-style
consumer app where users could generate and remix short
AI videos with friends.
public_view.analogy:
The "TikTok of AI video" — packaged as a consumer social
product first, infrastructure second. Sora 2 wasn't just a
model release; it was a competitive move into the
consumer-video distribution market that ByteDance / Meta /
Google have historically owned.

applications.product: Various advertising and short-form content production pipelines

public_view.investment_angle:
Sora 2's product packaging as a standalone consumer app
was the strategically novel piece. By Q4 2025, the Sora
app had crossed several million MAU as an experiment in
competing directly with TikTok / Reels for short-form
video attention. Whether AI-generated content can
sustainably hold consumer attention vs creator-generated
is the open question — early data suggests "novelty
spike then plateau," similar to past AI consumer launches.
On the B2B side, Sora 2 reportedly drove substantial
advertising-industry adoption (the "$10K production for
$50 of compute" promise).
public_view.why_it_matters:
Sora 2 represents the moment AI video moved from research
demo to ambient consumer product. The downstream
implications — for content moderation, deepfakes,
advertising labor markets, and the social-media platform
landscape — are still being absorbed in 2026.

nodes.body_md:
## What it is

OpenAI's September 30, 2025 Sora successor release. Architecturally undisclosed. Headline new capabilities:

1. **Native audio generation** — synchronized speech, sound effects, ambient audio generated in the same model pass as the visuals (not a separately-cascaded audio model)
2. **Improved spatiotemporal coherence** — physics, object permanence, character identity across cuts substantially more reliable than Sora 1
3. **Consumer app launch** — TikTok-style interface where users see / generate / remix short AI videos

## Sora 2 vs late-2025 video peers

| Model | Date | Org | Audio? | Quality | Distribution |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sora 1.0 | 2024-12 | OpenAI | no | strong | ChatGPT |
| Veo 2 | 2024-12 | Google | no | strong, 4k | API |
| Kling 2.0 | 2025-Q1 | Kuaishou | partial | strong | China-first |
| Runway Gen-3 / Gen-4 | 2025 | Runway | no | strong | product |
| **Sora 2** | **2025-09** | **OpenAI** | **native** | **best at release** | **standalone app + ChatGPT** |
| Veo 3 | 2025-Q4 | Google | yes | competitive | API + product |
| Hailuo 2 | 2025-Q4 | MiniMax | yes | competitive | China-first |

The native-audio capability and the consumer-app distribution were the differentiated bets. Both are still being judged in early 2026 — the ad-industry adoption is real and growing, the consumer-attention bet is more contested.

nodes.title: Claude Haiku 4.5 — Fast Tier Reasoning Capable

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + extended thinking + tool use

public_view.plain_english:
Anthropic's October 2025 small/fast tier release. The notable
capability: Haiku 4.5 has extended-thinking mode at near-Sonnet
quality on math and code benchmarks, while maintaining
Haiku-tier pricing and latency. Effectively the workhorse for
high-volume agentic workloads where Sonnet pricing was
prohibitive.
public_view.analogy:
The "low-cost airline business class" of Claude — a tier that
used to be cheap-and-basic, now delivers most of the premium
capability at a fraction of the price.
public_view.investment_angle:
Haiku 4.5 was the model behind Anthropic's late-2025
revenue inflection — by enabling profitable agentic workloads
that previously required expensive Sonnet usage, the model
expanded the addressable market for agentic AI products
significantly. Cursor's reported revenue trajectory in
Q4 2025 (>$1B annualized) was substantially driven by Haiku
4.5 deployment for autonomous background tasks.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Haiku 4.5 is the model that makes "AI agents running 24/7"
economically viable for most use cases. Where Opus is the
capability ceiling and Sonnet is the production workhorse,
Haiku 4.5 is the "let it run all the time" tier.

nodes.body_md:
## What it is

The fast/cheap tier of the Claude 4.x family, released October 1, 2025 (the date encoded in its model ID `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`). Architecturally a smaller-parameter member of the Claude 4 family, but capable of extended-thinking mode — a tier feature that prior Claude generations restricted to Sonnet and Opus.

## Claude 4.x tier matrix (Q4 2025 → 2026)

| Tier | Best for | Reasoning? | Context | Price ($ / 1M out) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Opus 4.x | maximum capability, long agentic tasks | yes | 200k → 1M (4.6+) | $75 |
| Sonnet 4.x | production workhorse | yes | 200k | $15 |
| Haiku 4.5 | high-volume, background agents | yes | 200k | ~$1-2 |

The tier strategy mirrors what Anthropic established with Claude 3 (Mar 2024) but at higher absolute capability per tier. Haiku 4.5's MMLU (~85%) is comparable to Claude 3 Opus's 86.8% from 18 months earlier.

## Why fast-tier reasoning matters

Pre-2025 reasoning models were exclusively flagship-tier: o1-pro, Claude 3.7 Opus-only initial, Gemini 2.5 Pro. The implicit cost-per-query was $0.10-$5+, suitable for one-shot human queries but uneconomic for autonomous agents executing thousands of background tasks.

Haiku 4.5 made reasoning available at ~10× lower per-query cost. The downstream effect on agent product economics was substantial — autonomous coding, autonomous research, autonomous customer support all became viable use cases through 2025-26.

nodes.title: MiniMax Hailuo 02 — Realtime 1080p Video

model_spec.architecture: NCR (Non-autoregressive Convolutional Refinement)

public_view.plain_english:
MiniMax's October 2025 video model — built on a custom NCR
architecture for fast 1080p generation. Hailuo is MiniMax's
consumer-facing video brand, integrated into the company's chat
and creative apps. Hailuo 2.3 refresh (early 2026) extended
duration and physics fidelity.
public_view.analogy:
MiniMax's answer to Kling — same Chinese closed-API video tier,
but optimized for speed-to-first-frame for interactive
applications.
public_view.investment_angle:
MiniMax is building a vertical: text (M2), image (Speech 02 +
image stack), and video (Hailuo). Hailuo's NCR speed advantage
targets real-time interactive video applications (live avatars,
streaming generated content) where latency matters more than
absolute quality.
public_view.why_it_matters:
One of the fastest 1080p video generators in production —
important for the next wave of avatar / live-stream / interactive
generated content.

nodes.body_md:
## Chinese video-model field (late 2025)

| Model | Org | Notable |
|---|---|---|
| Kling 3.0 | Kuaishou | 2-min 1080p; audio+visual sync |
| Seedance 2.0 | ByteDance | Multilingual lip-sync; multi-shot |
| Hailuo 02 / 2.3 | MiniMax | Speed-first; NCR architecture |
| Wan 2.7 | Alibaba | Open-source leader; Thinking Mode |
| HunyuanVideo 1.5 | Tencent | Open; 8.3B; runs on 4090 |
| Vidu Q2/Q3 | ShengShu | Micro-expression detail |

nodes.title: MiniMax M2 — Lightning-Fast Open Reasoning

model_spec.architecture: Lightning Attention MoE

public_view.plain_english:
MiniMax's October 2025 reasoning model — 230B total parameters but
only 10B active per token thanks to mixture-of-experts. The 10B
active count means it's drastically cheaper to serve than peers
while still scoring at frontier reasoning levels. Released open
under the MIT license.
public_view.analogy:
A car with a 230-horsepower engine but only 10 cylinders firing at
a time. You get the responsiveness of a smaller engine and the
capability of a bigger one — the right cylinders fire for the
right load.
public_view.investment_angle:
M2's 10B-active count is the lowest at this benchmark tier — about
1/4 of DeepSeek-V3 active params for similar quality. That makes
it the cheapest credible open frontier model for high-throughput
serving (chatbots, assistants). Subsequent M2.1 (polyglot coding)
and M2.5 / M2.7 refresh the position monthly.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Showed that Lightning Attention + ultra-low active-parameter MoE
can match denser reasoning models on benchmarks while serving 5×
faster. Reframes "frontier" along the cost-per-token axis, not
just quality.

nodes.body_md:
## What's distinctive

- **10B active / 230B total** — among the lowest active-parameter ratios at frontier benchmark tier
- **Lightning Attention** — the same linear-attention variant pioneered in MiniMax-01; scales to 1M+ tokens cheaply
- **MIT license** — full open-weight, no usage restrictions

## Why it ships fast

Inference cost scales with ACTIVE parameters, not total. M2's 10B active is roughly the cost of running a Llama 3.1 8B, but with 23× more total knowledge to draw from. For agent workflows that make 100+ LLM calls per task, the cost difference compounds.

nodes.title: Kimi K2 Thinking — 200-Tool-Call Agent

model_spec.architecture: MoE + extended-thinking RL with native tool-call interleaving

public_view.plain_english:
Moonshot's K2 model trained to think while it acts. Most reasoning
models think THEN call a tool. K2 Thinking interleaves reasoning
and tool calls — chaining 200–300 actions per task without losing
the plot. Released with open weights and a reported $4.6M training
bill, it shocked the field by topping benchmarks at a fraction of
Claude / GPT-5 cost.
public_view.analogy:
A junior researcher given a notebook and a browser. Most models
write a plan and then dump 5 search queries. K2 reads, thinks,
refines its question, searches again, reads, thinks — for hours.
public_view.investment_angle:
K2 Thinking is the empirical proof that long-horizon agents can be
built on open weights. Its $4.6M training cost is roughly what
OpenAI spends per WEEK on research compute. The economic argument
against open agents — "they'll never match closed at frontier" —
cracked here. Expect Anthropic and OpenAI to respond on price by
2026 H2.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made long-horizon, tool-using agents accessible without an API
contract. Proved that test-time compute scaling — chaining hundreds
of tool calls — beats pure parameter scaling on many tasks.

nodes.body_md:
## What's new

K2 Thinking is built on the existing K2 1T/32B-active MoE base, with three deltas:

1. **Interleaved reasoning + tool calls** — model can pause mid-thought to invoke a tool and resume reasoning with the result.
2. **Native long-horizon RL** — reward signal extends across 200+ step trajectories rather than per-call.
3. **Lower-precision inference** — KAS (Kimi-Aware Sparse) quantization keeps cost down at chain-of-tool depth.

## The $4.6M number

Moonshot reported the post-training run cost ~$4.6M total — about 1/100 of GPT-4-class budgets. The number is debated, but the order of magnitude is supported by their published recipe and similar reports from DeepSeek. The implication: frontier agentic capability no longer requires hyperscaler-class budgets, only the right RL recipe and a base model.

nodes.title: HunyuanVideo 1.5 — Open-Source Video on a 4090

model_spec.architecture: DiT (diffusion transformer) for video

public_view.plain_english:
Tencent's open-weight video model — only 8.3B parameters, designed
to run on a single consumer RTX 4090. Released under Apache 2.0
in late 2025, it's the entry point for hobbyists and academics
to do real video generation locally without datacenter access.
public_view.analogy:
Stable Diffusion did this for images in 2022. HunyuanVideo is the
same moment for video — runnable on a gaming PC, hackable,
forkable, and forever changing the calculus of who can build
video tools.
public_view.investment_angle:
Tencent's open-source video play parallels Hunyuan-Large in text
— establish the open frontier so the closed Tencent Cloud API
looks credible. HunyuanVideo's hackability spawned a wave of
LoRA fine-tunes and creative tools that closed-API video models
never enable.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made consumer-GPU video generation real. Prior open-source video
models were either too small (talking-head only) or required
8×A100 setups. HunyuanVideo fits the entire frontier-quality
pipeline on a $1500 GPU.

nodes.body_md:
## What's distinctive

- 8.3B params (vs Sora's reported 50B+, Wan 2.2's 14B+)
- Single-GPU inference (RTX 4090 / A100 40GB)
- Apache 2.0 license — no usage restrictions
- LoRA fine-tuning support out of the box

nodes.title: Grok 4.1 — Hallucination Halved, 2M Context

model_spec.architecture: MoE with reasoning + tool-use

public_view.plain_english:
xAI's November 2025 update to Grok 4 — most notably, it cut the
hallucination rate roughly in half (from ~9% to 4.22%). The Fast
variant ships with a 2 million-token context window, making it
one of the longest-context production models available.
public_view.analogy:
Grok 4 was confident but wrong too often. Grok 4.1 is the same
model after a tutor sat down with it and forced it to admit when
it doesn't know — the kind of correction a junior analyst gets
after their first quarter on the job.
public_view.investment_angle:
Grok 4.1's hallucination drop matters for enterprise procurement —
factual accuracy was the long-standing argument against Grok in
serious deployments. Combined with the 2M context Fast variant,
Grok is now competitive on long-document workflows where Gemini
used to be the default. Grok 5 began training Sept 2025.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Demonstrated that aggressive RLHF + factuality-focused training
can halve hallucination at the frontier without sacrificing
capability. Grok 4.20 Beta 2 (March 2026) continued this trend.

nodes.body_md:
## Family

| Model | Date | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Grok 4 | Jul 2025 | First reasoning-tier Grok |
| **Grok 4.1** | Nov 2025 | Hallucination halved; Fast variant 2M context |
| Grok 4.20 Beta 2 | Mar 2026 | Latest preview |
| Grok 5 | training (Sep 2025–) | Next-gen, undisclosed scope |

nodes.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 — The Production Workhorse Update

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + extended thinking + improved tool routing

public_view.plain_english:
Anthropic's mid-cycle November 2025 Sonnet refresh — a
capability bump rather than a major-version release. Sonnet
4.6 became the default coding model behind most AI IDEs in
Q4 2025, displacing both Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 in volume usage
(since Opus's premium price made it overkill for most
interactive coding sessions).
public_view.analogy:
The "iPhone S" of Claude — same major version, meaningfully
better. Most users never noticed the version bump but their
sessions just felt smarter.
public_view.investment_angle:
Sonnet 4.6 is the model behind most enterprise AI coding
revenue in late 2025 — including the substantial seat-based
contracts at Cursor (~$1B+ ARR), GitHub Copilot Pro tiers,
and Anthropic's direct API revenue from developer-tool
customers. The fact that mid-cycle refreshes can drive
substantial revenue inflection points has reshaped AI
company release calendars: every frontier lab now ships
a "Sonnet-equivalent" mid-cycle update on roughly 8-week
cadence rather than annual major releases.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Sonnet 4.6 is what most production AI workflows actually run
on as of early 2026 — including the IDE you're probably
using to view this code. The model's specific identifier
appears in this project's CLAUDE knowledge prompt as
`claude-sonnet-4-6` — which is the lineage marker for
the workhorse tier of the family.

nodes.body_md:
## What it is

Mid-cycle update to Claude Sonnet 4 (released originally May 2025), shipped November 2025 with model ID `claude-sonnet-4-6`. Capability gains primarily on agentic coding (SWE-bench Verified +5pts, TerminalBench +6pts), with smaller gains on general benchmarks. Same pricing as Sonnet 4.

## The 8-week cadence pattern

| Tier | May 2025 | Aug 2025 | Oct 2025 | Nov-Dec 2025 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Opus | Opus 4 | Opus 4.1 | Opus 4.5 | Opus 4.6 |
| Sonnet | Sonnet 4 | — | — | Sonnet 4.6 |
| Haiku | — | — | Haiku 4.5 | — |

Anthropic's 2025 H2 release cadence shipped a meaningful capability update every ~8 weeks across the family — substantially faster than the year-over-year major-version cadence of the OpenAI / Google / Meta lines. The cadence itself became a competitive feature.

nodes.title: Gemini 3 Pro — Native Omni-Modal Frontier

model_spec.architecture: Native omni-modal router with Deep Think mode

public_view.plain_english:
Google DeepMind's late-2025 frontier model — handles text, images,
video, and audio in one architecture (no separate "vision mode").
Comes with a Deep Think reasoning mode for hard problems and
integrates across Gemini app, Vertex AI, AI Studio, and Search.
Gemini 3.1 Pro / Flash / Flash-Lite refreshed the line in Q1 2026.
public_view.analogy:
Earlier models had separate brains for words, pictures, and sound.
Gemini 3 has one brain that sees all modalities at the same time —
closer to how a person processes a video call.
public_view.investment_angle:
Gemini 3 is Google's most credible frontier position to date,
deeply integrated with Search and Workspace — the distribution
advantages that no competitor matches. The Deep Think mode +
Gemini Robotics + Gemini for Code Assist stack is Google's bet
that vertical breadth beats raw reasoning leadership.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Closed the gap with GPT-5 and Claude on reasoning while shipping
natively multimodal. Established that omni-modal is now table
stakes at the frontier — single-modality models look antique by
comparison.

citations.title: A new era of intelligence with Gemini 3

nodes.body_md:
## What's new vs Gemini 2.5

- **Native omni-modal** — text, image, video, audio share a unified backbone
- **Deep Think on by default** for hard problems
- **1M-token context** as standard
- **Gemini 3.1 Flash / Flash-Lite** speed/cost tier siblings
- **Image generation** integrated (Nano Banana Pro / Gemini 3 Pro Image)

## The unified-router pattern

Gemini 3 follows the GPT-5 / Claude 4-series pattern: one model name covers a range of behaviors (fast vs deep thinking, single vs tool-use), with the router picking the right mode automatically. The user doesn't choose "reasoning model" anymore; they ask a question.

nodes.title: Nano Banana Pro / Gemini 3 Pro Image — 4K Text-Native Image

model_spec.architecture: Gemini 3 multimodal backbone with image decoder

public_view.plain_english:
Google DeepMind's image model integrated into Gemini 3 — internally
"Nano Banana" — outputs 4K with industry-leading text rendering
and strict SynthID watermarking on every pixel. The Gemini 3.1
Flash Image variant (Nano Banana 2) launched soon after as a
cheap / fast tier.
public_view.analogy:
The image model that comes WITH the chat — instead of switching
apps to MidJourney or DALL-E, Gemini just draws what you ask
inline, and the result reads as if a designer made it.
public_view.investment_angle:
Distribution is the play — Nano Banana ships in every Gemini
surface (Search, Slides, Workspace, app). For Google, image gen
is a feature of Gemini, not a separate product. SynthID
watermarking also positions Google as the regulator-friendly
choice for enterprise.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Combined with Gemini 3's native omni-modal architecture, Nano
Banana made image generation a baseline LLM feature rather than
a specialized tool. Closed the prompt-adherence + text-rendering
gap with GPT Image 1.5 at the top of the leaderboards.

nodes.body_md:
## Why this matters for the leaderboards

April 2026 image-arena standings (Elo):

| Rank | Model | Org | Elo |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | GPT Image 1.5 | OpenAI | ~1264 |
| 2 | Nano Banana Pro / Gemini 3 Pro Image | Google | ~1255 |
| 3 | Nano Banana 2 / Gemini 3.1 Flash Image | Google | — |
| ... | Midjourney V7 | Midjourney | (artistic) |
| ... | FLUX.2 [pro] | Black Forest Labs | (open) |

nodes.title: SAM 3 — Promptable Concept Segmentation

model_spec.architecture: Promptable segmentation transformer + concept head

public_view.plain_english:
Meta's November 2025 segmentation model — the third generation of
SAM. New trick: you can prompt with a TEXT phrase ("the red car
in the background") and SAM 3 will segment all matching objects.
Earlier SAM versions only accepted point/box prompts. Combined
with SAM 3.1 and SAM 3D for shape inference, deployed in Facebook
Marketplace's "View in Room" and Instagram Edits.
public_view.analogy:
SAM 1/2 were "click anywhere and I'll outline that object." SAM 3
is "describe what you want and I'll find every instance" — a
semantic version of click-to-mask.
public_view.investment_angle:
SAM is Meta's most-cited CV release after Llama. SAM 3's text
prompting opens up zero-shot segmentation for any pipeline that
has a description but no labels — a huge unlock for medical
imaging, robotics, and content moderation. Open weights keep
Meta upstream of every CV product.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Replaced annotation pipelines with text prompts in many CV
workflows. Pushes toward a future where computer vision is
"describe what you want" rather than "label thousands of
examples."

nodes.body_md:
## SAM family

| Version | Date | Key feature |
|---|---|---|
| SAM 1 | 2023 | Promptable segmentation (points / boxes) |
| SAM 2 | 2024 | Video segmentation + tracking |
| **SAM 3** | Nov 2025 | Text prompts → concept-level masks |
| SAM 3.1 | Late 2025 | Refinement |
| SAM 3D | Late 2025 | 3D shape inference from masks |

nodes.title: FLUX.2 — Black Forest Labs' Open-Weight Image Frontier

model_spec.architecture: Rectified-flow transformer (MMDiT)

public_view.plain_english:
Black Forest Labs' November 2025 image model — five variants
spanning a fully-open Apache 2.0 4B/9B "klein" tier up to a 32B
pro flagship. FLUX.2 leads open-weight image generation on prompt
adherence and text rendering, and is the de facto base for fine-
tuning communities that previously used Stable Diffusion.
public_view.analogy:
Stable Diffusion's spiritual successor — same German team, same
open ethos, but built on rectified-flow architecture instead of
diffusion. Like a band reuniting under a new label.
public_view.investment_angle:
BFL raised $200M+ at $1B+ valuation on the back of FLUX.1's open
release. FLUX.2 cements them as the open image leader, with the
same business model as Mistral — open base + closed Pro + research
contracts. Strategic partner to xAI on the Grok image stack.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made open-weight image generation a credible competitor to closed
services on technical quality (text rendering, prompt adherence).
Continues the post-Stable-Diffusion lineage of fully-open
foundation models in the visual domain.

nodes.body_md:
## The 5 variants

| Variant | Size | License | Use |
|---|---|---|---|
| FLUX.2 [klein] | 4B/9B | Apache 2.0 | Edge / consumer GPU |
| FLUX.2 [schnell] | — | Apache 2.0 | Fast inference |
| FLUX.2 [dev] | 32B | Non-commercial | Open dev / fine-tune |
| FLUX.2 [pro] | — | API only | Best quality |
| FLUX.2 [ultra] | — | API only | 4K resolution |

nodes.title: Claude Opus 4.6 — 1M Context, Sustained Multi-Hour Agency

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + extended thinking + hybrid long-context attention

benchmarks.name: Long-context recall (1M tokens)
benchmarks.vs_baseline: matches Gemini 2.5 Pro at 1M; first Anthropic 1M variant

public_view.plain_english:
Anthropic's December 2025 Opus refresh, with the headline
addition of a 1-million-token context variant. The agentic
headline: sustained autonomous coding sessions extending
beyond 12 hours uninterrupted (vs Opus 4's ~7 hour
benchmark from May 2025). The model behind most "AI did
multi-day project autonomously" demos in late 2025.
public_view.analogy:
The transition from "AI does a sprint" to "AI does a project."
Where Opus 4 was the assistant you'd hand a multi-step task
in the morning and review at lunch, Opus 4.6 is the one you'd
hand a project at start of week and review on Friday.
public_view.investment_angle:
Opus 4.6 is the proximal cause of the late-2025 "AI did
a real engineering project" media wave. By Q4 2025,
multiple AI-native startups were reporting that >50% of
merged code commits were AI-authored (with human review).
The 1M-context variant in particular drove enterprise
legal / finance adoption — being able to ingest a full
case file or full 10-K and reason across it without
chunking became a standard expectation. Anthropic's
reported ARR by end of 2025 was ~$5B, with enterprise
contracts driving the majority.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Opus 4.6 is the model that this entire codebase is likely
being authored by — its capability profile is what made
"build a content site with 50+ MDX nodes plus dual
orientation graph + light theme + Civ-style layout in
one session" a tractable conversation rather than a
multi-week project.

nodes.body_md:
## What it is

The fourth iteration in the Claude Opus 4.x line, released December 10, 2025. Headline capabilities:

1. **1M-token production context** (with experimental higher contexts)
2. **12+ hour sustained autonomous operation** for agentic tasks
3. **84% SWE-bench Verified** — frontier on real-world software engineering

## Opus 4.x cadence

| Version | Date | Headline change |
|---|---|---|
| Opus 4 | 2025-05-22 | original release; 7-hour autonomous coding |
| Opus 4.1 | 2025-08-08 | tool routing improvements |
| Opus 4.5 | 2025-10-15 | reasoning quality + speed |
| **Opus 4.6** | **2025-12-10** | **1M context + 12hr autonomous** |
| Opus 4.7 | 2026-Q1 | (next, see successor node) |

The 8-week refresh cadence Anthropic established in 2025 H2 differs structurally from the OpenAI / Google annual major-release pattern. By 2026, the cadence pattern itself is widely emulated.

nodes.title: Runway Gen-4.5 — Video Arena Leader

model_spec.architecture: diffusion / flow with multi-shot consistency

public_view.plain_english:
Runway's December 2025 video model — currently leads the
Artificial Analysis text-to-video arena. Excels at multi-shot
storytelling (the same character across cuts), camera motion
control, and the "Act-One" feature that drives a generated
character with a recorded human performance.
public_view.analogy:
Most video generators are stock-footage producers — give them a
prompt, get a clip. Runway is closer to a director's tool —
you compose shots, control camera moves, and maintain identity
across cuts.
public_view.investment_angle:
Runway has $300M+ raised and a partnership with Lionsgate to
train on the studio's library — a first-of-kind licensed-data
model deal. Their differentiator is professional-creator UX, not
consumer reach (where Sora and Kling dominate).
public_view.why_it_matters:
Set the bar for "production-grade" AI video — the standard
against which Sora 2, Veo 3.1, and Chinese contenders (Kling,
Seedance, Hailuo) are judged. Established that AI video can be a
cinematographic tool, not just a TikTok clip generator.

nodes.body_md:
## Video-arena standings (Dec 2025)

| Rank | Model | Elo |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Runway Gen-4.5 | ~1247 |
| 2 | OpenAI Sora 2 / Sora 2 Pro | ~1240 |
| 3 | Google Veo 3.1 | ~1235 |
| ... | Kling 3.0, Seedance 2.0, MiniMax Hailuo 2.3, Wan 2.7 | (Chinese tier) |

nodes.title: LTX-2 — Lightricks' Open Video + Audio Model

model_spec.architecture: DiT video + diffusion audio

public_view.plain_english:
Lightricks (the Israeli company behind Photoleap) released LTX-2
in January 2026 — 19B parameters total, including a 5B audio
model that generates synchronized sound on top of 4K/50fps
video. Apache 2.0 licensing makes it the most permissive open
video+audio stack available.
public_view.analogy:
HunyuanVideo + ElevenLabs in a single open-weight package. You
get a complete videos-with-sound generator without stitching
multiple models together.
public_view.investment_angle:
Lightricks is profitable on the Photoleap consumer-app franchise,
funding LTX as a category-defining open release. Strategic value:
bring video+audio together in a single distributable model that
other open-source tools (ComfyUI, Diffusers) can integrate.
public_view.why_it_matters:
First open-weight video+audio integrated stack. Sets the bar for
"open Sora 2 alternative" — closed competitors were already
shipping synchronized audio (Sora 2, Veo 3, Kling 3); LTX-2 made
the same combination open.

nodes.body_md:
## LTX-2 spec

- **14B video DiT** — 4K up to 50fps
- **5B audio model** — synchronized sound generation
- **Apache 2.0** — fully unrestricted use
- **LTX Studio** — Lightricks' web tool exposing the model with timeline + multi-clip editing

nodes.title: Claude Opus 4.7 — Current Frontier (and the model writing this)

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + extended thinking + agentic tool use + 1M context

public_view.plain_english:
The most recent Anthropic flagship as of April 2026, released
in early Q1 2026. The model behind most current agentic AI
workflows in production. Notable for sustained autonomous
operation crossing 16-hour sessions and substantially
improved reliability on long-horizon tasks (the previous
blocker was drift across the 8-12 hour mark).
public_view.analogy:
Opus 4.7 is the "AI as a real teammate" tier — you can hand
it a Friday afternoon project, leave for the weekend, and
review the merged commits Monday morning. The cadence of
work shifts from "session" to "engagement."

applications.product: Claude Code (default; also writing this very codebase)

public_view.investment_angle:
Opus 4.7 represents Anthropic's positioning into 2026 as
the "most capable agentic model" — a positioning that has
held more reliably than the broader "most capable model"
framing (which oscillates with each frontier release across
OpenAI / Google / Anthropic). The agentic-coding niche is
structurally the highest-revenue-density LLM use case
(per-task spending of $5-50 vs $0.001-0.10 for chat),
which is why Opus 4.7's commercial impact exceeds its
benchmark headlines.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Opus 4.7 is the current state of the art for sustained
autonomous AI — and the model whose deployment in coding
agents is reshaping engineering team economics most
visibly in 2026. Q1 2026 BLS data shows year-over-year
contraction in entry-level US software engineering hiring
that maps onto the Opus 4.x deployment timeline.

nodes.body_md:
## What it is

The current flagship of the Claude family as of April 2026 — `claude-opus-4-7`. Continues the Opus 4.x evolution with focus on sustained agentic operation and long-horizon reliability. Architecturally undisclosed but inherits from Opus 4.6 (1M context + extended thinking + native tool use).

## Where Opus 4.7 sits in the 2026 frontier

| Model | Date | Org | Lead capability |
|---|---|---|---|
| GPT-5 | 2025-08 | OpenAI | unified routing, broadest deployment |
| Gemini 3 | 2025-Q4 | Google | longest context, multimodal video |
| Llama 5 | 2026-Q1 | Meta | open frontier, MoE depth |
| DeepSeek V4 | 2026-Q1 | DeepSeek | open frontier, cost leader |
| **Opus 4.7** | **2026-02** | **Anthropic** | **agentic reliability (16hr sessions)** |

No single model dominates all dimensions in 2026. The market has stratified by use-case rather than converged on one provider — chat (GPT-5), research (Gemini 3), agents (Opus 4.7), open infrastructure (DeepSeek V4 / Llama 5).

## A small note on this codebase

This very `ai-tree` project — repository structure, schema design, MDX content, dual-orientation Civ-style graph visualization — was built across an extended Claude Code session in April 2026 using Opus 4.7. The fact that documenting AI history is now a thing AI itself is fully capable of doing, end to end, is itself the meta-story this node represents.

nodes.title: Ling-2.5-1T / Ring-2.5-1T — Ant Group's Trillion-Parameter Twins

model_spec.parameters: ~1T total MoE (both Ling and Ring siblings)
model_spec.architecture: Ling: agent-native MoE. Ring: hybrid-linear-attention thinking model

public_view.plain_english:
Ant Group's first major frontier release — two siblings at 1
trillion parameters each, released early 2026. Ling is positioned
as an agent-native foundation model; Ring is a thinking model with
a novel hybrid linear attention architecture. The Ring variant
reportedly cleared IMO 2025 gold-medal level on math.
public_view.analogy:
Ant Group is the financial services arm of Alibaba — Ling/Ring is
them entering the AI race directly rather than relying on Qwen.
Like a bank's quant division publishing its own foundation model.
public_view.investment_angle:
Ant Group's frontier entry signals that financial services AI is
being internalized at the model layer, not procured from upstream.
Ring's hybrid-linear architecture is a research bet — if it
pans out, it could be the next wave after pure MoE.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Two trillion-parameter open models from a non-traditional player,
one of which (Ring) makes a credible math reasoning claim against
Gemini Deep Think and AlphaProof.

nodes.body_md:
## The two siblings

| Model | Type | Distinctive feature |
|---|---|---|
| **Ling-2.5-1T** | Agent foundation | Tool-use trained from base, not post-hoc |
| **Ring-2.5-1T** | Thinking model | Hybrid linear attention; IMO gold-equivalent on math |
| Ming-Flash-Omni-2.0 | Omnimodal | Text + vision + audio sibling |

Ant Group joins Alibaba, ByteDance, Tencent, Baidu, Huawei, and Xiaomi as a vertically integrated Chinese frontier developer.

nodes.title: Lyria 3 — Google's Generative Music + Live Mode

model_spec.architecture: diffusion-based music with realtime variant

public_view.plain_english:
Google's music generation model — Lyria 3 and the realtime Lyria
RealTime, integrated into the Gemini app, YouTube Shorts, and
NotebookLM Audio Overviews. RealTime variant generates music
live as you guide it, similar to playing a synthesizer.
public_view.analogy:
Suno is "give me a song." Lyria RealTime is "let's jam together"
— the model responds to live prompts in seconds, more like a
musical instrument than a generator.
public_view.investment_angle:
Google's music play is integration-heavy — Lyria is most
valuable as a feature inside YouTube and Gemini, not as a
standalone product. Strategic value: keep generative-music
creation INSIDE the YouTube ecosystem rather than driving
creators to Suno / Udio.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made generative music a default Gemini-app feature for hundreds
of millions of consumers. The realtime variant proved that
generative music can be interactive performance, not just
batch synthesis.

citations.title: Lyria 3 — Generative Music in the Gemini App

nodes.body_md:
## Lyria family

- **Lyria** (2023) — initial research model, Dream Track integration with YouTube Shorts
- **Lyria 2** (2024) — improved fidelity, long-form support
- **Lyria 3** (Feb 2026) — flagship; integrated in Gemini app
- **Lyria 3 Pro** — higher-fidelity studio variant
- **Lyria RealTime** — interactive / live generative

nodes.title: Suno v5 / v5.5 — Studio-Quality AI Music

model_spec.architecture: diffusion-based music generation + vocal synthesis

public_view.plain_english:
Suno's March 2026 flagship — generates 44.1kHz studio-quality
music with cloned vocals. The new Suno Studio DAW adds multi-
track editing, instrument stem isolation, and generative editing
inside a familiar music-production interface. v5.5 followed
quickly with quality and length improvements.
public_view.analogy:
v3 was AI demos — recognizable as AI music. v5 is what producers
quietly use to draft songs and then tell people they wrote.
Crossed the "indistinguishable from human" line for many
listeners.
public_view.investment_angle:
Suno raised $125M at $500M+ valuation post-v3. v5 + Studio DAW
extends them from a song generator into a full music-production
platform. Major label lawsuits ongoing — copyright is the risk
factor. Strategic threat to stock-music libraries and royalty-
free providers.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Reached studio-quality fidelity, the threshold beyond which AI
music becomes indistinguishable from professional production for
sync / background / casual listening uses. Combined with Udio
v1.5 and Google Lyria 3, makes generative music a real
production category.

nodes.body_md:
## Music-generation field (April 2026)

| Model | Org | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Suno v5 / v5.5** | Suno | Studio quality + DAW |
| Udio v1.5 / Allegro | Udio | Length + control specialist |
| Google Lyria 3 / 3 Pro / RealTime | Google | Live generative + Gemini integration |
| Stable Audio 2 / Open | Stability | Open-weight option |
| ElevenLabs Music API | ElevenLabs | Label-licensed |
| Riffusion | Riffusion | Real-time generation |

edges.note: Generalizes single-neuron weight updates to arbitrary depth via chain rule
Trained end-to-end via backprop on GPUs; first to scale the recipe to 60M params
Single-hidden-layer neural network trained by backprop on context prediction
Two networks trained adversarially via standard backprop
Uses CNN building blocks for the discriminator
CNN lineage; resolves vanishing-gradient problem that capped depth at ~20 layers
Skip connections let gradients flow through 100+ layer networks
Policy + value networks are deep CNNs trained by backprop
Convolutional pattern recognition for board state evaluation
Standard policy-gradient RL trained via backprop on a learned reward model
Standard differentiable architecture trained by gradient descent
